title: Noccaea alpina
intro: Noccaea alpina, also known as alpine penny-cress, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Noccaea alpina include glucosinolates, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Noccaea alpina is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and is used to support skin health, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Noccaea alpina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is also known as Alpine Penny-cress. The genus Noccaea is a group of plants closely related to the genus Thlaspi, but is now considered distinct. Noccaea alpina is native to mountainous regions of Europe. It is a small, annual plant with white or pink flowers.
uses: Noccaea alpina, also known as alpine penny-cress, has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. In folk medicine, Noccaea alpina has been employed to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Its extracts have also been investigated for their antioxidant and antiviral properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Noccaea alpina for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Noccaea alpina contains a range of active constituents, including glucosinolates such as glucobrassicin and neoglucobrassicin. These compounds are known for their potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Research also suggests the presence of isothiocyanates, which have been linked to their antibacterial and anticancer effects.
